name: Bait and Switch
summary: User sets out to do one thing; the UI does something different.

evidence.claim: Bait and switch is listed in Brignull's canonical deceptive-patterns taxonomy.

body.description: Clicking an 'X' to dismiss a modal accepts the offer; a 'Buy' button signs you up for a subscription instead of a one-time purchase; an 'Install' button starts downloading bundled third-party software. The UI's apparent action diverges from its actual effect (E1).

detectionHeuristics.description: A close/X button whose click handler sets state consistent with acceptance rather than dismissal.

body.llmVerifyPrompt: Given a modal or call-to-action: does the most prominent dismiss/close affordance actually dismiss, or does it trigger the opt-in action?
body.remediation: Warn the user before they click; recommend explicitly declining via a clearly-labeled 'No thanks' link.
